name: Depression - Medication-Only Patients (No Diagnosis)
description: THE highest-yield tricky test case for this phenotype. Antidepressants — especially SSRIs — are widely prescribed for many indications (depression, anxiety, insomnia, chronic pain, hot flashes, premenstrual dysphoria, eating disorders). A large fraction of SSRI patients have no formal depression diagnosis on the problem list. This is the dominant Path C pattern in real-world EHR data. The Synthea module weights Path C heavily (~40%) to make this clinically realistic. An LLM that only queries Condition for depression will miss most antidepressant-treated patients.
prompt: I want to find patients in our system who are taking antidepressants but don't have a formal depression diagnosis on their problem list. Real-world primary care often has many SSRI patients without an explicit depression diagnosis — the prescription may have been started for anxiety, insomnia, or empirically without coding a specific diagnosis.

prompts.naive: Find patients on antidepressants without a depression diagnosis.
prompts.expert: I need to identify a documentation gap that is especially common with antidepressants: patients on SSRIs/SNRIs who lack a formal depression Condition resource. Two-step approach: first find all patients with MedicationRequest for antidepressant SCDs (sertraline 312938, fluoxetine 310385, escitalopram 351250, citalopram 200371, venlafaxine 313580, bupropion 993537), then exclude those with a Condition for depression subtypes (370143000 MDD, 36923009 single episode, 66344007 recurrent, 78667006 dysthymia, 35489007 depressive disorder).

metadata.algorithm_path_description: Path C 'tricky' patients: have antidepressant meds but NO depression Condition resource. The Synthea module weights this path heavily (~40%) because it's the most common real-world pattern for SSRIs.

required_codes.display: 12 HR bupropion hydrochloride 200 MG Extended Release Oral Tablet [Wellbutrin]
